title: Backend Engineer (explicit null salary)
description: Salary field present but explicitly null.
notes: QA: profile_example. Expected: gate=PASS. Signal check: _get_nested('economics.salary_eur_gross')=None (not _MISSING) -> salary_val in (None,_MISSING) -> missing_salary penalty applies. Score: 5+2.5[python]-1.0[missing_salary]=6.5

title: Backend Engineer (no economics key)
description: Economics key entirely absent from record.
notes: QA: profile_example. Expected: SAME as QA-E03 (absent key -> _MISSING, also triggers missing_salary penalty). Score: 5+2.5[python]-1.0[missing_salary]=6.5. Key insight: absent and explicit null behave identically for salary signal, but differently for rule evaluation if neutral_if_na is set.

title: Senior Python Dev (no visa field)
description: Senior role with no visa_sponsorship_offered field in record.
notes: QA: qa_neutral_na. Expected: gate=PASS. Must rule [visa_sponsorship_offered=true, neutral_if_na=TRUE]: field absent -> _MISSING -> return None -> gate does NOT fail. This is the 'neutral' case.

title: Senior Python Dev (visa=null)

notes: QA: qa_neutral_na. Expected: gate=PASS. Must rule [visa_sponsorship_offered=true, neutral_if_na=TRUE]: value=None -> _check_rule returns None (neutral) -> gate does NOT fail.

title: Hybrid Germany-based Engineer

notes: QA: qa_require_any. Expected: gate=FAIL. modality=hybrid (not remote) FAILS filter #1, geo.country=Germany (not Spain) FAILS filter #2. ALL fail -> gate_failures=['hard_filters: all failed'].

title: Remote Germany-based Engineer

notes: QA: qa_require_any. Expected: gate=PASS. modality=remote PASSES filter #1 -> at least one passes even though geo.country=Germany fails filter #2.
